title: Default ERD View and Guidance for Large Object Sets
story: As a DataTools Pro user with many objects in my Dictionary, I want to set a custom view as my default ERD view and receive guidance when my 'All' view becomes too complex, so that the ERD is immediately useful when I navigate to it.
description:
Two improvements to the ERD default view experience:

### 1. Set Default View
Users can designate any custom ERD view as the default. When the ERD page loads, it opens to the default view instead of "All". The view action bar shows "Set as default view" for non-default views and "Unset default view" for the current default.

### 2. Large Object Set Guidance
When the "All" view contains more than 10 objects and no default view has been set, a guidance message appears above the ERD: "You have more than 10 objects in your dictionary. We recommend using views to streamline visibility and understanding of your entity relationships. **Learn how to create ERD views**." Clicking "Learn" opens a sidebar with tutorial content.
acceptance_criteria: Users can set any custom ERD view as the default view
The default view loads automatically when navigating to the ERD page
The 'Set as default view' option appears for non-default views
The 'Unset default view' option appears for the current default
When 'All' view has more than 10 objects and no default is set, a guidance message appears
The guidance message links to a 'Learn' sidebar with tutorial content on creating views
Users with 10 or fewer objects see no guidance message
ux_changes: New 'Set as default view' / 'Unset default view' action in the view management bar. Guidance banner for large object sets recommending views. 'Learn' link opens sidebar with tutorial content.
middleware_changes: Logic to persist and retrieve the default view setting. Object count check to trigger the guidance message when 'All' view exceeds 10 objects.
data_model_changes: Default view preference stored per team, associated with the ERD view record.
implementation_notes: The guidance message only appears when: (1) the selected view is 'All', (2) the object count exceeds 10, and (3) no default view has been set. There was a bug during testing where the message wasn't appearing for the reporter's account despite having more than 10 objects — this was debugged and resolved.
comment_summary: Developer implemented set/unset default view and the 10+ objects guidance message with learn sidebar. Reporter verified most features but didn't initially see the >10 objects message, which was debugged.
